properties.name: Renderers must produce byte-identical output
properties.description: Deterministic renderers must emit byte-identical Excalidraw and Markdown artefacts for the same Neo4j graph state across runs and machines.

properties.name: Tool runs on host; never containerised
properties.description: The analyst-tool must run directly on the analyst host machine and must not be containerised, since it shells out to the local claude CLI.

properties.name: Pacer enforces working-window for claude calls
properties.description: All programmatic claude subprocess calls must respect a Pacer-enforced working-window to control rate and avoid runaway invocations.

properties.name: No private info in public repo
properties.description: The public NaCl repo must not contain client project names, local absolute paths, dump metadata, or operational anecdotes; a canary grep runs before every release.

properties.name: Project registry is single source of truth
properties.description: ~/.nacl/projects.json is the only authority for project root and active project; all resolution flows through it.

properties.name: Pacer wrapper mandatory for claude subprocess
properties.description: All programmatic claude -p invocations must go through the itsalt-pinch Pacer wrapper; direct child_process.spawn(claude) is forbidden.

properties.name: Atomic tmp+rename writes for boards
properties.description: All board-file writes must be atomic via temp file + rename to avoid partial writes corrupting analyst state.

properties.name: Graceful degradation when Neo4j unreachable
properties.description: Search, diagnostics, and non-graph features must continue to function when Neo4j is unreachable; only graph-dependent operations should fail with a clear error.

properties.name: Serve board bytes when present
properties.description: GET /api/v1/boards/:name returns the .excalidraw scene JSON when the file exists, else 404.

properties.name: Atomic save with hash update
properties.description: PUT /api/v1/boards/:name persists the new scene atomically via tmp+rename and updates the content hash.
